title: Who I work best with
summary: I'm most effective when the context fits how I work: strategy and execution in one place, clarity over buzzwords, and room to reduce complexity.

items.title: Product and engineering leaders
items.content: You want a senior partner to structure the roadmap, challenge the architecture, and deliver, not just execute tickets.

items.title: Teams with legacy or tangled systems
items.content: You want to reduce debt, clarify structure, and stabilize without a full rewrite.

items.title: Teams with a strong CMS or editorial footprint
items.content: You want decoupled options, a sustainable block editor and editorial model (including Gutenberg where WordPress is already the backbone), and APIs your team can evolve, without a bespoke integration snowstorm every launch.

items.title: Multi-brand or multi-market companies
items.content: You need to manage complexity, consistency, and performance without adding unnecessary layers.

items.title: Small teams (2–4 devs) under pressure
items.content: You need clear prioritization, solid technical standards, and a structured framework to ship cleanly.

items.title: Startups and scale-ups
items.content: You want a responsible technical voice, able to hold the line from strategy to delivery.

profiles-heading: Profiles that tend to fit best
situations-quiz-lede: Find the situation page that matches your context in two questions.
